_note: AI-Assisted — Human Review Required. This mapping was co-developed with Brazilian regulatory expertise. Graziela Peduti (Head of Global Regulatory Intelligence, NexTrial.ai) reviewed ANVISA, CEP/CONEP, and LGPD requirements. Verify current regulation status before relying on this mapping.
language_note: Brazilian regulations are authoritative in Portuguese. This mapping is in English for international accessibility. The Portuguese originals govern in all cases of conflict.

regulations.title: Lei de Pesquisa Clínica / Clinical Research Law
regulations.relevance: Brazil's primary clinical research law. Governs all aspects of clinical trial conduct including ethics approval, informed consent, post-trial access, and AI use in clinical contexts.

key_requirements.requirement: Research must be approved by an accredited Ethics Committee (CEP) and, where required, by CONEP before initiation.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify CEP approval documentation present and current before site activation

key_requirements.requirement: Post-trial access to beneficial interventions must be planned and documented before trial initiation.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify post-trial access plan present in trial documentation
key_requirements.note: This is a distinguishing requirement of Brazilian law with no direct US equivalent. Must not be overlooked in multi-jurisdiction trials.

key_requirements.requirement: Informed consent must be obtained in language accessible to the research participant, with the right to withdraw at any time.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify consent document language level and withdrawal rights statement

key_requirements.requirement: Data from clinical research must be stored in Brazil or in countries with equivalent data protection, subject to LGPD requirements.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify data residency statement in trial protocol or data management plan

regulations.title: Resolução da Diretoria Colegiada 945 — Clinical Trial Dossier Requirements
regulations.relevance: Governs the structure and content of clinical trial dossiers submitted to ANVISA.

key_requirements.requirement: The clinical trial dossier must include: protocol, investigator brochure, informed consent form, qualification of investigators, and site qualification documentation.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify all required dossier sections present and complete

key_requirements.requirement: All dossier documents submitted to ANVISA must be in Portuguese or accompanied by certified Portuguese translation.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify Portuguese language version present for all required dossier documents
key_requirements.note: AI-generated documents must be reviewed by a qualified bilingual regulatory professional before submission. Machine translation alone does not satisfy this requirement.

key_requirements.requirement: Changes to the protocol after ANVISA approval require a protocol amendment submission with updated risk-benefit assessment.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify protocol version in dossier matches ANVISA-approved version

regulations.title: CEP/CONEP Ethics Review — Foundational Guidelines
regulations.relevance: Governs ethics review by CEP (site-level) and CONEP (national-level). International and multicenter trials require CONEP review in addition to CEP.

key_requirements.requirement: CEP approval required for all research involving human beings at the institution.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify CEP approval certificate present and within validity period

key_requirements.requirement: CONEP review required for: research involving indigenous populations; research in areas of national biosecurity; international collaborative research; research with foreign genetic material.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Evaluate whether trial characteristics trigger CONEP review requirement
key_requirements.note: Most international sponsor trials require CONEP review. Verify applicability case-by-case.

key_requirements.requirement: Ethics approval must be documented with CEP registration number (CAAE — Certificado de Apresentação de Apreciação Ética).
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify CAAE number present in ethics documentation

regulations.title: Conselho Federal de Medicina — AI in Medical Practice
regulations.relevance: CFM Resolution 2.454/2024 governs the use of AI in medical practice in Brazil. It has direct implications for AI-assisted patient eligibility determinations and any AI output that influences a medical decision in a patient encounter.

key_requirements.requirement: AI systems used in medical practice must be validated and must not replace the physician's clinical judgment.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify system documentation demonstrates physician oversight for all medical determinations
key_requirements.gate_3_implication: Any medical determination (eligibility, safety assessment, clinical judgment) requires Level 3 Attestation under SAID-SPEC-001 regardless of protocol configuration. This is a jurisdiction-specific override.

key_requirements.requirement: The physician retains full ethical and legal responsibility for decisions made with AI assistance.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify liability documentation present in site agreement
key_requirements.gate_3_implication: Level 3 attestation PI statement must explicitly reference clinical responsibility acceptance

key_requirements.requirement: AI-assisted decisions must be documented with sufficient information to reconstruct the basis for the decision.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify proof certificate architecture satisfies reconstruction requirement
key_requirements.note: This is the Brazilian regulatory basis for the proof certificate requirement. The proof certificate architecture is directly responsive to this provision.

regulations.citation: LGPD — Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (Lei 13.709/2018)
regulations.title: General Data Protection Law
regulations.relevance: Brazil's data privacy law. Clinical trial data involving Brazilian participants is subject to LGPD. Applies to AI systems processing participant data.

key_requirements.requirement: Processing of health data (a sensitive data category under LGPD) requires explicit consent or one of the enumerated legal bases.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify data processing consent basis documented in trial protocol or data management plan

key_requirements.requirement: Data subjects have the right to request review of automated decisions that affect their interests.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify participant data rights documentation includes Art. 20 review right
key_requirements.note: The proof certificate architecture supports this right — the certificate documents the basis for the automated determination and can be provided to the participant on request.

key_requirements.requirement: Data controllers and processors must adopt technical and administrative security measures to protect personal data.
International transfer of personal data is only permitted to countries with adequate protection level or with specific safeguards.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If trial data transferred internationally: verify transfer mechanism documented

regulatory_positioning_note.description: Brazil's regulatory environment is characterized by detailed, prescriptive requirements across multiple bodies (ANVISA, CEP/CONEP, CFM, ANPD). AI systems operating in Brazilian clinical trials must satisfy requirements across all applicable bodies simultaneously. The proof certificate architecture was specifically designed to satisfy CFM 2.454's reconstruction requirement — which is more prescriptive than comparable US or EU requirements at this time.
regulatory_positioning_note.strategic_note: Brazilian regulatory requirements for AI in medical contexts (CFM 2.454) are among the most operationally specific globally. Compliance here establishes a high-water mark that satisfies most other jurisdictions' requirements by extension.

_changelog.changes: Initial regulatory mapping — developed with Brazilian regulatory expertise
